replies.author: @RaikenXion
replies.text:
Keep doing your thing Theory, and never rush anything. You don't have to worry about strict schedules or such things. When it's ready it's ready. 

I've been working on my own little  project, it's a redux of the sequel trilogy. I plan to one day publish it somewhere on the Internet in a comic book form, because drawing/Art is my skill-fortay. But I'm doing it all on my own, the writing and drawing the scenes in cells. It's hard work, but I don't rush, and because I have bipolar I'm up & down in my mindset, so some days it's abit of a struggle for me to get motivated. 

I've even had it where I've left working on it for about a week or two, taken a good break but then I get like this kind of writer's fear, to  go back to it. It's hard keeping myself focus, and like staying in that zone, Inside that universe, where I've escaped this REAL WORLD I live in day to day; but where I shut myself off in my own little workshop (which is either my bedroom or the sideroom downstairs), and I'm then like Sebastian in the never ending story film, where he's up in the school attic, immersed into that book, with the Realm of Fantasia lol. 

That's where I struggle sometimes to keep myself in, but when I'm there dude, it's magical because then my Immagination just wonders and it's like I can sort of "Think" how those characters think and behave. 

So lets keep doing our thing and stay Creative, I wish you all the best Theory!

replies.text: Of all the characters to survive. Mace is top of the list. A vengeful Jedi story against Vader. Gives me chills.

replies.author: @TheLostSheep578
replies.text: He makes so much more sense to have survived then Palpatine it is insane. Imagine him surviving and a story about his witnessing the fall of the Jedi, perhaps he blames himself for not getting the job done and his struggles to fight back the Empire that seeks the erradication of the last Jedi. Perhaps he meets someone falls in love and has a family and the son of his son is none other than Finn who ends up as a storm trooper with the force and he is the lead of the Sequels as he fights back against the First Order

replies.author: @blackdeku8917
replies.text: As a kid it always annoyed me that more Jedi’s didn’t survive order 66. Especially mace windu, if anything it makes the most since if he comes back. Obviously he would have to die before new hope…although windu losing to Vader…..that’s a little debatable

replies.author: @chewy99.
replies.text: @TheLostSheep578 No way bro Palpatine is literally #1 on top of the list of people likely to return from apparent death. He had contingencies upon contingencies and of course cared about his own life more than anyone has ever cared about their own, because he’s perfectly selfish and anything that does not serve him in some way is useless. He literally came back and had tons of plans and contingencies in *both* continuities. Again I say, if anyone in Star Wars was ever to come back from apparent death, it is this most evil, most powerful, most selfish of dark siders.

replies.author: @TheLostSheep578
replies.text: ​@chewy99.I heavily disagree. It completely disvalues Anakin's prophecy to destroy the sith and bring balance to the force. If Palpatine somehow survives like he did then that means Anakin never actually fulfilled his prophecy. I'd much rather they introduce a new Sith like they tried to do with Snoke. Someone who inherited Palpatine's will and desires and seeks to recreate the Sith to do so than they just bring Palpatine back to life.

author: @OdellAtkinson
text:
I was the Voice Director for Hazbin Hotels pilot. You need a voice director. Good solid takes , but lacking depth from what I've seen here. Someone who understands the theatrical beats of the script and can tie it in with the planned boards to make sure the beats are beatin'.

Looking forward to the future of your work!! I know Star Wars is safer in your hands, sir!!
From one anti-Hollywooder to another.

But hey if your going for a more cartoon vibe then ok I doke.
